# nurah

`nurah` is an R package designed as a research compendium to evaluate
the feasibility of estimating mortality in humanitarian crises. It
leverages statistical modelling, directed acyclic graphs (DAGs), and
simulations to address data availability challenges, producing robust
and actionable mortality estimates.

## Overview

`nurah` facilitates:

1.  Defining causal structures for mortality estimation using DAGs.
2.  Simulating crisis-affected population data based on defined DAGs.
3.  Estimating indirect mortality through Bayesian hierarchical models
    informed by the causal DAG.

## Installation

Install the latest version from GitHub:

``` r
# install.packages("devtools")
devtools::install_github("jameel-institute/nurah")
```

## Usage

### Defining a DAG

Start by defining your causal structure with predictors and an outcome
(mortality):

``` r
library(nurah)

nodes <- c("Nutrition", "\"Food insecurity\"", "Mortality")
edges <- data.frame(
  from = c("\"Food insecurity\"", "Nutrition"),
  to = c("Nutrition", "Mortality")
)

dag <- define_dag(nodes, edges)
visualise_dag(dag)
```

![](reference/figures/README-unnamed-chunk-2-1.png)

### Specifying Parameters

You can add specific parameter values (effect sizes and lags) to your
DAG:

``` r
parameters <- data.frame(
  from = c("\"Food insecurity\"", "Nutrition"),
  to = c("Nutrition", "Mortality"),
  effect_size = c(-0.5, 1.2),
  lag = c(30, 0)
)

dag <- define_dag(nodes, edges, parameters)
```

### Using Checchi et al. (2017) DAG

A core inspiration for `nurah` is the DAG from Checchi et al. (2017).
You can quickly use this predefined DAG:

``` r
dag <- checchi_2017_dag()
visualise_dag(dag, label_size = 4.5)
```

![](reference/figures/README-unnamed-chunk-4-1.png)

``` r
# Dummy parameters for demonstration
parms <- dummy_checchi_2017_parameters()
dag <- checchi_2017_dag(parameters = parms)
head(parms)
#>                                                      from
#> 1 Exposure to armed attacks or mechanical force of nature
#> 2 Exposure to armed attacks or mechanical force of nature
#> 3 Exposure to armed attacks or mechanical force of nature
#> 4 Exposure to armed attacks or mechanical force of nature
#> 5 Exposure to armed attacks or mechanical force of nature
#> 6 Exposure to armed attacks or mechanical force of nature
#>                                           to effect_size lag
#> 1                        Forced displacement         0.2   0
#> 2          Interruption of chronic treatment         0.3  14
#> 3           Sexual and gender-based violence         0.4  14
#> 4            Burden and typology of injuries         1.2   0
#> 5 Mental health and psychosocial functioning         0.2  30
#> 6        Humanitarian public health services        -0.5   0
```

### Simulating Data

Use your DAG to simulate crisis data with defined spatial and temporal
structures:

N.B. WARNING - Currently this does not set any values in your DAG if the
parent nodes have no values! (TODO List)

``` r
sim_data <- simulate_crisis_data(
  dag,
  start_date = "2020-01-01",
  n_periods = 6,
  resolution = "month",
  spatial_structure = data.frame(
    country = "X", region = c("A","B"), district = c("A1","B1")
  ),
  initial_population = 10000,
  noise_level = 0.8
)
```

### Fitting Hierarchical Model

Fit a Bayesian hierarchical model to estimate indirect mortality using
simulated data:

``` r
fit <- fit_dag(
  data = sim_data,
  dag = dag,
  outcome = "Population mortality",
  spatial_levels = c("region", "district"),
  iter = 1000,
  chains = 2
)

summary(fit)
```

### Other Features To Describe/Finish/Test:

- Simulating missingness in data observation (MCAR, MAR, MNAR)
  (<https://github.com/jameel-institute/nurah/tree/missingess/proxies>)
- Simulating proxies - data that is hoped to reflect a node but may have
  different effect size or directionality to node
  (<https://github.com/jameel-institute/nurah/tree/missingess/proxies>)
- Aggregation (how data collected at different spatial or temporal
  resolution could be aggregated and impact on model identifiability)
- Custom model formula into model fit
- Model summary plots and summaries for assessing identifiability
  against DAG
- Identify where to pull in
  [http://robindenz1.github.io/simDAG/index.html](http://robindenz1.github.io/simDAG/index.md)
  or keep distinct

## Getting Help

For issues, feature requests, or support, please open an issue on
GitHub:

- [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/jameel-institute/nurah/issues)

## References

- Checchi et al. (2017). [“Public health information in crisis-affected
  populations: a review of methods and their use for advocacy and
  action”](NA). *Lancet*

# Package index

## All functions

- [`aggregate_simulated_data()`](https://ojwatson.github.io/nurah/reference/aggregate_simulated_data.md)
  : Aggregate Daily Simulation Data to Periods
- [`apply_mask()`](https://ojwatson.github.io/nurah/reference/apply_mask.md)
  : Apply Missingness Mask to Simulated Data
- [`checchi_2017_dag()`](https://ojwatson.github.io/nurah/reference/checchi_2017_dag.md)
  : Corrected Checchi et al. (2017) DAG Definition
- [`compute_recovery_metrics()`](https://ojwatson.github.io/nurah/reference/compute_recovery_metrics.md)
  : Compute Recovery Metrics for Simulation Study
- [`create_scenario_grid()`](https://ojwatson.github.io/nurah/reference/create_scenario_grid.md)
  : Create Default Scenario Grid
- [`define_dag()`](https://ojwatson.github.io/nurah/reference/define_dag.md)
  : Define a Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) for mortality prediction
- [`dummy_checchi_2017_parameters()`](https://ojwatson.github.io/nurah/reference/dummy_checchi_2017_parameters.md)
  : Generate Dummy Parameters for Checchi et al. (2017) DAG
- [`fit_dag()`](https://ojwatson.github.io/nurah/reference/fit_dag.md) :
  Fit DAG-based Bayesian Hierarchical Model
- [`nurah-package`](https://ojwatson.github.io/nurah/reference/nurah.md)
  [`nurah`](https://ojwatson.github.io/nurah/reference/nurah.md) :
  nurah: Testing causal models of mortality in crisis-affected
  populations
- [`pool_imputation_fits()`](https://ojwatson.github.io/nurah/reference/pool_imputation_fits.md)
  : Pool Results from Multiple Imputation Fits
- [`predict_deaths()`](https://ojwatson.github.io/nurah/reference/predict_deaths.md)
  : Extract Predicted Deaths from Fitted Model
- [`run_simulation_study()`](https://ojwatson.github.io/nurah/reference/run_simulation_study.md)
  : Run Simulation Study for Mortality Estimation
- [`save_figs()`](https://ojwatson.github.io/nurah/reference/save_figs.md)
  : Save Figures
- [`setup_spatial_structure()`](https://ojwatson.github.io/nurah/reference/setup_spatial_structure.md)
  : Set up Spatial Structure for Simulation
- [`setup_temporal_structure()`](https://ojwatson.github.io/nurah/reference/setup_temporal_structure.md)
  : Set up Temporal Simulation Structure
- [`simulate_crisis_data()`](https://ojwatson.github.io/nurah/reference/simulate_crisis_data.md)
  : Simulate Crisis Data Based on a DAG Model
- [`simulate_missingness()`](https://ojwatson.github.io/nurah/reference/simulate_missingness.md)
  : Simulate Missingness in Predictor Variables
- [`simulate_node()`](https://ojwatson.github.io/nurah/reference/simulate_node.md)
  : Simulate a Single DAG Node
- [`simulate_population_dynamics()`](https://ojwatson.github.io/nurah/reference/simulate_population_dynamics.md)
  : Simulate Population Dynamics with IDP Flows
- [`summarize_study_results()`](https://ojwatson.github.io/nurah/reference/summarize_study_results.md)
  : Summarize Simulation Study Results
- [`tidy_dagitty_coords()`](https://ojwatson.github.io/nurah/reference/tidy_dagitty_coords.md)
  : Tidy a dagitty object with optional coordinates
- [`topological_sort_dag()`](https://ojwatson.github.io/nurah/reference/topological_sort_dag.md)
  : Topologically Sort DAG Nodes
- [`visualise_dag()`](https://ojwatson.github.io/nurah/reference/visualise_dag.md)
  : Visualise a Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) with Rounded‐Corner Boxes

### All vignettes

- [nurah: Simulating and Estimating Indirect Mortality in Crisis
  Settings](https://ojwatson.github.io/nurah/articles/nurah_vignette.md):
